close_reason: Fixed by correcting the property test. strinc does NOT preserve ordering when one key is prefix of another - this is an inherent limitation of prefix range semantics. Added documenting test and updated property test to only check equal-length keys.
description:
Property test failure in Bedrock.KeyTest at apps/bedrock_core/test/bedrock/key_test.exs:226

**Test**: 'property ordering properties strinc preserves ordering (when both valid)'

**Failed after 78 successful runs with values:**
- key1: "," (comma, ASCII 44)
- key2: <<44, 187>> (comma followed by byte 187)

**Assertion failure:**
```
assert strinc1 < strinc2
left:  "-"
right: ",\xBC"
```

**Reproduction seed**: 81475

The strinc function should preserve ordering such that if key1 < key2, then strinc(key1) < strinc(key2). This property is violated for certain binary inputs near byte boundaries.
title: strinc property test fails: ordering not preserved for binary keys
